note_data.title: Probability and Statistics
keyPoints: The solution(s) of a pair of linear and/or non-linear equations correspond to the coordinates of the intersection point(s) of the graphs
A pair of simultaneous linear equations is of the form ax + by = p, cx + dy = q, where a, b, c and d are constants, x and y are variables to be determined
There is usually one solution to a pair of simultaneous linear equations
The methods most commonly used to solve simultaneous linear equations are elimination and substitution
In elimination, the coefficient of one of the variables is made the same in both equations. The equations are then either added or subtracted to form a single linear equation with only one variable
In substitution, a variable is made the subject of the chosen equation. This equation is then substituted into the equation that was not chosen to solve for the variable
A non-linear equation is not of the form ax + by = p
The method most commonly used to solve simultaneous non-linear equations is substitution

For substitution method: Step 1: Use the linear equation to express one of the variables in terms of the other. Step 2: Substitute it into the non-linear equation. Step 3: Substitute the value(s) obtained in Step 2 into the linear equation to obtain the value of the other variable
When solving simultaneous non-linear equations, substitute the values of x into the linear equation to obtain the corresponding values of y

definitions.definition: A pair of simultaneous linear equations is of the form ax + by = p, cx + dy = q, where a, b, c and d are constants, x and y are variables to be determined
A non-linear equation is not of the form ax + by = p
The coefficient of one of the variables is made the same in both equations. The equations are then either added or subtracted to form a single linear equation with only one variable
A variable is made the subject of the chosen equation. This equation is then substituted into the equation that was not chosen to solve for the variable
The solution(s) of a pair of linear and/or non-linear equations correspond to the coordinates of the intersection point(s) of the graphs

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Show all working steps clearly and give the final answer
Show the method and working to reach the numerical answer
Work out the answer showing clear mathematical reasoning

modelAnswer.question: A cricket team has 8 Pakistani players and 3 foreign players. If 3 players are selected at random to give interviews, find the probability that exactly 2 are Pakistani players.
modelAnswer.answer:
Total players = 8 + 3 = 11
Total ways to select 3 players = ¹¹C₃ = 11!/(3!×8!) = 165
Ways to select exactly 2 Pakistani players = ⁸C₂ × ³C₁ = 28 × 3 = 84
Probability = 84/165 = 28/55

commonMistakes: Not using the linear equation to express one variable in terms of the other when solving non-linear equations
Forgetting to substitute values back into the linear equation to find corresponding values
Not simplifying equations properly during elimination or substitution
examinerTips: Use the linear equation to express one of the variables in terms of the other when solving simultaneous non-linear equations
Substitute the values of x into the linear equation to obtain the corresponding values of y
formulas: P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A ∩ B)
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Tree diagram showing all possible outcomes with probabilities on each branch, multiplying along branches for individual outcomes and adding different paths for final probability
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q7 (6 marks) — they asked students to calculate probability using tree diagrams for conditional events with replacement
Featured in O/N 2022 P1 Q12 (4 marks) — students had to find mean and standard deviation from a frequency table with grouped data
Appeared in M/J 2022 P2 Q8 (7 marks) — required drawing cumulative frequency curve and finding median and quartiles
Tested in O/N 2021 P2 Q6 (5 marks) — students calculated probability of combined independent events and drew Venn diagrams

workedExample.question: A bag contains 5 red balls and 3 blue balls. Two balls are drawn without replacement. Calculate the probability that both balls are red. (3 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify this as probability without replacement, so probabilities change after first draw
Step 2: P(1st ball red) = 5/8 (5 red balls out of 8 total)
Step 3: P(2nd ball red | 1st red) = 4/7 (4 red balls left out of 7 total remaining)
Step 4: P(both red) = P(1st red) × P(2nd red | 1st red) = 5/8 × 4/7 = 20/56 = 5/14
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct first probability (5/8), 1 mark for correct conditional probability (4/7), 1 mark for correct final answer in simplest form

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused independent and dependent events' — always check if events affect each other and adjust probabilities accordingly
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to use class midpoints when calculating mean from grouped frequency tables' — use midpoint of each class interval, not the class boundaries
Examiner Report 2021: 'Poor accuracy in reading values from cumulative frequency curves' — use a ruler and read carefully from the curve, not the plotted points
note_data.keyDiagram: Tree diagram showing all possible outcomes with probabilities on branches and final outcomes at endpoints. Must include: clear branching structure, probability fractions/decimals on each branch, final probabilities calculated by multiplying along paths, all probabilities checking to sum to 1. For cumulative frequency: smooth curve (not joining plotted points) with clearly labeled axes and scale, showing upper class boundaries on x-axis.
note_data.quickRecap: Simultaneous equations can be solved using elimination or substitution methods. For linear equations, either method works well, but for non-linear equations, substitution is most commonly used. The key is to use the linear equation to express one variable in terms of another, then substitute into the non-linear equation. Always substitute back to find corresponding values of both variables.
